preview_text:
The model rates Iran as a clear favorite, assigning a 65% win probability compared to New Zealand's 10%. That 55-percentage-point gap reflects a substantial edge to the Iranian side across the forecast distribution.

The draw holds material weight at 25%, suggesting the match carries genuine competitive tension despite Iran's favoritism. The win probabilities spread unevenly—Iran's lead is steep, but New Zealand's single-digit chance leaves room for an upset, and the draw probability indicates neither a blowout nor a lopsided mismatch is the modal outcome.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge notably. Books and crypto markets cluster around 51% for Iran and 20–21% for New Zealand, sitting roughly 14 percentage points below the model's Iran estimate and 10 points above its New Zealand floor. The draw forecasts align closely across all sources (25–28%). The dashboard makes no claim to resolve which assessment is more accurate; the divergence warrants attention from users making decisions based on these inputs.
